title: Hash and equality contract

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Hash and equality contract; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Hash and equality contract
sources.claim: Hash tables require equality and hashing to agree so equal keys produce equal hashes and stable key state while stored.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Hash tables require equality and hashing to agree so equal keys produce equal hashes and stable key state while stored.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A case-insensitive key hashes the same normalized representation used by equality. Your goal is to design and test a key equality and hashing policy in the selected language. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Array representation and operations; Linked lists; Correctness and counterexamples. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Hash tables require equality and hashing to agree so equal keys produce equal hashes and stable key state while stored. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, implement or select a table with explicit hash, equality, capacity, and ownership; in C++, unordered containers require compatible hash/equality and stable keys; in Java, HashMap/HashSet depend on equals and hashCode; in Python, dict/set require hashable keys with consistent __eq__ and __hash__.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Collisions are expected, mutable keys can become unreachable, and iteration order is generally not a sorting guarantee. The draft therefore rejects: A good hash function eliminates every collision.

includes: Map equal keys to equal hashes and keep keys stable while stored.
Design and test a key equality and hashing policy in the selected language.

In C, implement or select a table with explicit hash, equality, capacity, and ownership; in C++, unordered containers require compatible hash/equality and stable keys; in Java, HashMap/HashSet depend on equals and hashCode; in Python, dict/set require hashable keys with consistent __eq__ and __hash__.
excludes: Collisions are expected, mutable keys can become unreachable, and iteration order is generally not a sorting guarantee.

outcomes: Design and test a key equality and hashing policy in the selected language.

examples.title: Hash and equality contract: language-neutral trace
examples.situation: A case-insensitive key hashes the same normalized representation used by equality.
walkthrough: Step 1 — State the abstract input and invariant: A case-insensitive key hashes the same normalized representation used by equality.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Collisions remain possible; the essential contract is that equal keys have equal hashes.
examples.result: The conceptual result follows because Collisions remain possible; the essential contract is that equal keys have equal hashes.

examples.title: Hash and equality contract: implementation transfer

walkthrough: Step 1 — Keep the same specification and invariant across all four languages.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: In C, implement or select a table with explicit hash, equality, capacity, and ownership; in C++, unordered containers require compatible hash/equality and stable keys; in Java, HashMap/HashSet depend on equals and hashCode; in Python, dict/set require hashable keys with consistent __eq__ and __hash__.
Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: Collisions are expected, mutable keys can become unreachable, and iteration order is generally not a sorting guarantee.
examples.result: Concept mastery transfers, while implementation evidence remains language-specific.
